evidence: the emergence, adoption and evolution of new technologies, including...AI technologies, have required us to invest resources to adapt our existing products and services, and we expect to continue to make such investments, which could be material
We have invested significant resources into the development of electronic trading systems and expect to continue to do so
in response to the proliferation of AI-enabled fraud and ransomware attacks...we have...allocated additional resources for business continuity
as we expand the development and incorporation of AI technologies in our business processes, services or products, including in connection with our OneGS 3.0 initiative

We or our third-party vendors, clients or counterparties have in the past developed or incorporated and may in the future develop or incorporate AI technology in certain business processes, services or products
our competitors may be more timely or successful in developing or integrating AI technologies to increase their productivity and reduce their costs or to provide better transaction execution
the Firmwide Artificial Intelligence Risk and Controls Committee, which oversees risks associated with the use of AI, reports to the Firmwide Technology Risk Committee

AI models, particularly generative AI models, may produce output...that is incorrect, that result in the release of private, confidential or proprietary information, that reflect biases...infringe on the intellectual property rights of others

The legal and regulatory environment relating to AI is uncertain and rapidly evolving...These evolving laws and regulations have in the past required and may in the future require changes in our implementation of AI technology and increase our compliance costs

limitations: ai_investment_intensity: No dollar amounts, GPU counts, or quantified AI-specific capex disclosed in the excerpts; investment language is qualitative and forward-looking ('could be material'). Score 3 reflects named initiative (OneGS 3.0) and stated resource allocation without quantification.

ai_narrative_concreteness: AI use cases are named at the initiative level (OneGS 3.0, business processes, electronic trading) but no deployment metrics, production status details, or quantified outcomes are disclosed in the excerpts. Score 3 reflects named but unquantified initiatives.

ai_risk_disclosure_depth: Score 5 supported by a named governance body (Firmwide AI Risk and Controls Committee), specific risk mechanisms (model bias, IP infringement, regulatory compliance costs, generative AI fraud), and evidence of active mitigation (phishing training, resource reallocation). Risk-section material is present and substantive.
